title: List A - List of Certain Antimicrobial Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients - Canada.ca
content:
## List A: List of certain antimicrobial active pharmaceutical ingredients
List A names certain antimicrobial active pharmaceutical ingredients that are important in human medicine. We have put a number of measures in place to help limit the development of resistance to these medically-important antimicrobials (MIAs) when used in animals. Learn more about List A .
Access archived versions of this list.
Drugs containing any of the following:
Including (but not limited to)
Effective Date (yyyy-mm-dd)
Beta-lactamase    inhibitors
Benzofuran    naphthyridines
Cephalosporins -    first generation
Cefradine (Cephradine)
Cephalexin (Cefalexin)
Cephalothin (Cefalotin)
Cephapirin (Cefapirin, Cefapyrin)
Cephalosporins - second    generation
Lorcarbef (Loracarbef, Lorcacarbef)
Cephalosporins -    third generation
Cephalosporins -    fourth generation
Cephalosporins -    fifth generation
Bicyclomycin (Bicozamycin)
DNA    polymerase inhibitors
Trovafloxacin (Alatrofloxacin)
Contezolid acefosamil
Sulopenem etzadroxil
Benzathine penicillin
Clomethocillin (Nafcillin)
Penethamate hydriodine
Penicillin G (Benzylpenicillin)
Penicillin V (Phenoxymethylpenicillin)
Colistin (Polymyxin E)
Enramycin (Enduracidin)
Rifampin (Rifampicin)
Spiropyrimidinetriones
Phthalylsulfathiazole
Sulfachloropyridazine
Sulfamethoxypyridazine
Tetracyclines –    newer generation
Therapeutic agents for tuberculosis
4‐Aminosalicylic Acid    (Para-aminosalicylic acid)
Calcium aminosalicylate
Triazaacenaphthylenes
